name: plugin-dev
description: Lean, security-aware authoring kit for Claude Code AND Claude Cowork plugins, with a balanced deterministic/LLM split: a deterministic bash validation suite (scripts/validate-plugin.sh orchestrating per-domain validators that emit a shared JSON finding contract) plus scaffolders (scripts/scaffold-{plugin,skill,command,hook}.sh) own mechanical structure and checks, while the LLM lane (skills + agents) owns judgment — description leak/injection review, triggering quality, and design. Skills for plugin structure / skill / command / agent / hook / MCP authoring, Cowork-first development with multilingual triggers and connector-aware enrichment, cross-host subagent scaffolding (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, OpenCode), leak-audit and best-practices-sync passes, plus skill-workshop which mines session JSONL history (via the session-analyzer Haiku subagent) for skill candidates; agents for validation, skill review, agent generation, and session mining; create-plugin command for guided scaffolding. Covers 2026 hook events, Cowork primitives (connectors / scheduled tasks / routines), and known Cowork bugs.
